(Emphasis supplied)
58. It is thus a settled position of law that the plaint may be amended 
at any stage of the proceedings to enable the plaintiff to seek an 
alternative relief, including that of refund of earnest money, and the 
courts have been vested with wide judicial discretion to permit such 
amendments. However, under Section 22 of the 1963 Act, the courts 
cannot grant such relief suo moto, since the inclusion of the prayer 
clause remains a sine qua non for the grant of such a relief. In other 
words, when an “appropriate case” exists for seeking the said relief 
under this provision, it must be specifically sought either in the original 
plaint or by way of an amendment. This has been emphatically held 
by this Court in Desh Raj v. Rohtash Singh, reported in (2023) 3 
SCC 714. The relevant observations are reproduced hereunder:
